title: First Curve
brief: Heading commands snap to straight legs. This time fly a smooth arc: after taking control, send ONE flight command carrying a Curvature and let Flight Autonomy steer the curve to the terminal. Keep the curvature inside the airframe's turn limit.
teaches: CurveFollowing is a distinct flight mode selected by a Curvature on the flight command. FA validates the curvature against the body's minimum turn radius — too tight and it REJECTS with PERFORMANCE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED. A valid curve runs itself: FA reports START_CURVE_FOLLOWING → CURVE_IN_PROGRESS → CURVE_COMPLETED.

objective._doc: curve-complete: FA's curve must reach CURVE_COMPLETED and the vehicle holds the terminal zone at (500,500), altitude 3000. The Mule's 6 deg/tick rate easily flies a 0.002 curvature arc from heading 0 (north) round to the north-east terminal.
